Name: Application Layer Protocol
Description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols to avoid detection or network filtering by blending in with existing traffic. Often used for Command and Control (C2).

Name: Non-Standard Port
Description: Adversaries may communicate using a protocol that does not follow the expected standards for the port to bypass network filtering. Ports like 4444 or 1337 are highly indicative of this evasion technique.

Name: Unix Shell
Description: Adversaries may abuse Unix shell commands (bash, sh) and utilities (nc, python3) for execution and reverse shell connections routing to external IPs.

Name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
Description: Adversaries may attempt to take advantage of a weakness in an Internet-facing computer or program using software, data, or commands in order to cause unintended behavior.

Name: Network Service Discovery
Description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services listening on remote hosts and local network infrastructure devices to identify potential vulnerabilities or map the Honeypod.

Name: Network Denial of Service
Description: Adversaries may perform Network Denial of Service (DoS) attacks to degrade or block the availability of targeted resources to users.
